lede: A look at July 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alou Diarra and Rembrandt, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Psy
events.desc: South Korean rapper Psy releases his hit single Gangnam Style.

events.title: Caspian Airlines Flight 7908
events.desc: Caspian Airlines Flight 7908 crashes near Jannatabad, Qazvin, Iran, killing 168.

events.title: X (social network)
events.desc: Twitter, later one of the largest social media platforms in the world, is launched.

events.title: Space Race
events.desc: Space Race: Apollo–Soyuz Test Project features the dual launch of an Apollo spacecraft and a Soyuz spacecraft on the first Soviet-United States human-crewed flight. It was the last launch of both an Apollo spacecraft, and the Saturn family of rockets.

events.title: Nicosia
events.desc: In Nicosia, Cyprus, Greek junta-sponsored nationalists launch a coup d'état, deposing Makarios III and installing Nikos Sampson as the president of Cyprus.

events.title: Nobel Prize
events.desc: Eighteen Nobel laureates sign the Mainau Declaration against nuclear weapons, later co-signed by thirty-four others.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: The Holocaust: Nazi Germany begins the deportation of 100,000 Jews from the occupied Netherlands to extermination camps.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Second Battle of the Marne begins near the River Marne with a German attack.

events.title: Seattle
events.desc: In Seattle, Washington, William Boeing and George Conrad Westervelt incorporate Pacific Aero Products (later renamed Boeing).

events.title: French Revolution
events.desc: French Revolution: Gilbert du Motier, Marquis de Lafayette, is named by acclamation Colonel General of the new National Guard of Paris.

births.name: Carl Bildt
births.line: Swedish politician and diplomat, Prime Minister of Sweden

births.name: Aníbal Cavaco Silva
births.line: Portuguese economist and politician, 19th President of the Portuguese Republic

deaths.text: Hugo von Hofmannsthal, Austrian author, poet, and playwright
Anton Chekhov, Russian playwright and short story writer
Vladimir the Great, Grand prince of Kievan Rus' (born c. 958)

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bernhard II, Margrave of Baden-Baden
Christian feast day: Dispersion of the Apostles (formerly by the Catholic Church)
